title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple reflects on her companion, Miss Knight, a fifty-six-year-old woman with bronchitis who is described as kind but patronizing. Despite Dr. Haydock’s warning that Miss Knight must sleep in a room with someone due to her illness, Miss Marple finds the arrangement tolerable, though she dislikes being called "dear" by her.

After a nap, Miss Knight prepares to go for a walk. Miss Marple provides her with an extensive list of errands to ensure she stays away for a significant amount of time, including checking on curtains at a London store, buying blue yarn from Mrs. Willett, getting blackcurrant lozenges at a drugstore, exchanging a book at the library, and inquiring about a hand-operated whisk mixer at Harritz’s. Miss Marple intentionally includes the mixer, knowing such an item does not exist in the local market.

Once Miss Knight departs, Miss Marple waits for her to be sufficiently occupied before changing into outdoor clothes and heading out for her own walk. As she walks, Miss Marple observes Mr. Lecock pruning roses and reflects on the simple-minded nature of Miss Knight, comparing her to a woman named Emily Waters. Miss Marple follows a path through the vicarage garden, passing an iron gate and a bridge over a stream to reach a nearby development.
